summary: Plan-only PR inventory classification for 10 open PR candidates. No GitHub mutations are planned. Four security-sensitive PRs are routed to central security handling; five ordinary PRs are kept non-mutating because they need normal review/proof/branch work; one maintainer-owned draft needs human follow-up.

actions.reason: Security-sensitive memory/session-boundary PR is out of scope for backlog cleanup automation.
Keep open as an independent PR; dirty branch and review-bot history block any merge or close-style action.
Keep open independently; proof failure blocks merge and fixed-by-candidate closeout, but does not justify closure.
Related to the open Feishu card issue, but review changes are still required, so no merge or close action is safe.
Security-linked cron run-name scoping PR must be routed to central OpenClaw security handling.
Keep open independently; missing proof blocks merge or fixed-by-candidate closeout.
Trusted sender provenance changes touch a security boundary and should be routed outside ProjectClownfish cleanup.
Maintainer-owned draft with active technical correctness concern requires human/author follow-up, not automated closeout.
Broad security-boundary feature PR is out of scope for ProjectClownfish mutation and should be routed centrally.
Related to the open Feishu slash-command issue, but dirty merge state and proof failure block any close, merge, or fixed-by-candidate action.

needs_human: #89292: maintainer-owned draft PR has active human technical concern about object identity and is waiting on author.
